metadata.title: Chapter Quizzes
metadata.description: End-of-chapter quizzes for all 18 chapters of the Roof Systems Field Guide

questions.text: The granular surface on a composition shingle is composed of:

answers.t: Mineral granules embedded in the asphalt

questions.text: The most common composition shingle profile installed today is:
Expected NorCal field lifespan for architectural composition shingles is:
Granules in the gutters are most commonly a sign of:

answers.t: Aging or weathering of the shingles

questions.text: Inadequate attic ventilation accelerates shingle aging primarily by:

answers.t: Trapping heat that ages shingles from below

questions.text: 'Lifetime warranty' on shingles typically means:

answers.t: A non-prorated period followed by prorated coverage that decreases over time

questions.text: A 12-year-old architectural shingle roof with a single failed pipe boot, otherwise good, is best addressed with:

answers.t: Repair of the pipe boot, with annual maintenance recommended
answers.e: Localized failure on roof with significant remaining life.

questions.text: Algae streaking on the north slope is best treated with:

answers.t: Algaecide treatment with attention to underlying conditions

questions.text: A 23-year-old three-tab roof with widespread granule loss exposing asphalt, multiple lifted shingles, and active leaks is best addressed with:
When a customer asks 'how long will my new shingle roof last?', the honest response is:

answers.t: Provide a range based on system, conditions, and observed condition — never a specific year-count

questions.text: TPO seams are joined using:

answers.e: TPO is thermoplastic; seams are welded.

questions.text: USA Flat Roof's standard TPO specification is:
The most common color of TPO membrane in NorCal commercial is:
Where do most TPO leaks originate?
T-joints (where three TPO sheets meet) are noteworthy because:

answers.t: They're high-failure locations requiring careful welding
answers.e: Three-sheet meeting points are stress concentrations.

questions.text: Fastener bumps under the membrane along seams typically indicate:

answers.e: Mechanical attachment shows fastener bumps; expected.

questions.text: A 16-year-old TPO with sound seams, no chronic ponding, addressable details is best addressed with:

answers.t: Silicone restoration coating if it qualifies
answers.e: Mid-life with sound substrate is restoration window.

questions.text: TPO membrane under HVAC equipment can fail because of:

answers.t: Damage from foot traffic and equipment maintenance
answers.e: Trade damage at HVAC is common.

questions.text: A property manager wants TPO replacement on a chronically ponded roof. The honest response is:

answers.t: Quote TPO overlay without addressing slope
Quote TPO with tapered insulation, or recommend SPF as alternative for ponding correction

questions.text: Comparing three TPO proposals at very different prices, the rep should:

answers.t: Help compare scopes — mil thickness, prep, fastener spec, warranty tier — not just prices
Tell customer the cheapest is the worst

questions.text: Silicone coating is best understood as:

answers.t: An engineered liquid-applied membrane that bonds to the existing roof and forms a continuous waterproof layer

questions.text: Silicone coatings excel at:

answers.t: UV resistance and ponding water tolerance

questions.text: A silicone coating warranty typically covers:

answers.t: The coating product, but not the substrate underneath

questions.text: Disqualifying conditions for silicone restoration include:

answers.t: Wet insulation, chronic ponding, widespread substrate failure

questions.text: Fabric reinforcement in silicone is typically applied at:
When applying silicone over an existing acrylic-coated roof, the rep should:

answers.t: Verify compatibility per manufacturer specifications and escalate if uncertain

questions.text: A property owner with a chronically ponded roof asks for silicone restoration. The honest response is:

answers.t: Decline restoration, explain why, recommend alternatives like SPF or replacement with tapered insulation

questions.text: Service life extension from silicone restoration on qualifying substrate is typically:
When a coating fails in ponded areas before warranty period, this typically indicates:
A repeat customer pushes for silicone on a non-qualifying substrate. The most appropriate response is:

answers.t: Provide because they're a repeat customer
Maintain the honest decline, explain disqualification, recommend honest alternatives

questions.text: Roof rejuvenation is appropriate for which roof type?
Rejuvenation works at the chemical level by:

answers.t: Adding a continuous waterproof layer on the surface
Replacing oils that aging shingles have lost

questions.text: USA Flat Roof positions Fresh Roof rejuvenation as:

answers.t: Proactive maintenance applied while the roof is still in good shape

questions.text: After rejuvenation, the visible appearance of the roof typically:

answers.t: Looks largely the same as before
answers.e: Treatment absorbs into shingles; subtle visible change.

questions.text: A roof outside the qualifying age window for rejuvenation should be:
When asked 'how many years will rejuvenation add?', the most accurate answer is:

answers.t: A specific number based on warranty length
The manufacturer's published extension range with notation that actual results depend on roof condition

questions.text: Disqualifying conditions for rejuvenation include:

answers.t: Roof in mid-life with intact granules
Active leaks, widespread granule loss, multiple damaged shingles, soft deck

questions.text: When a customer says 'is rejuvenation a scam?', the most appropriate response includes:

answers.t: Acknowledging the concern, explaining the chemistry, emphasizing the qualification process

questions.text: A homeowner says rejuvenation will save them from replacement entirely. The accurate response is:

answers.t: Explaining that rejuvenation extends life but doesn't eliminate eventual replacement
Telling them they'll never need replacement

questions.text: Fresh Roof rejuvenation works best when integrated into:

answers.t: A broader maintenance program — annual inspection, gutter service, minor repairs

questions.text: PVC and TPO seams are joined by the same method:
PVC is positioned by USA Flat Roof as:

answers.t: Premium alternative to TPO with chemical and grease resistance

questions.text: The primary aging mechanism in PVC is:

answers.t: Plasticizer migration leading to stiffening and brittleness

questions.text: A restaurant with significant kitchen exhaust most likely has PVC because:

answers.t: PVC's chemical and grease resistance significantly exceeds TPO

questions.text: When a rep cannot certify TPO vs PVC by appearance, the appropriate documentation is:

answers.t: Documenting visual indicators with notation that product type cannot be certified by appearance alone

questions.text: Plasticizer migration is most visible on PVC roofs that are approximately:
Standard PVC field lifespan in NorCal is approximately:
A 28-year-old PVC roof with widespread membrane stiffening, multiple cracked areas, and chronic ponding should be addressed with:
When silicone restoration is being applied over existing PVC, the critical step is:
A property manager compares a PVC and TPO proposal at significantly different prices. The rep should:

answers.t: Help the customer understand PVC and TPO are different products with different applications and warranties — not interchangeable

questions.text: USA Flat Roof installs which modified bitumen installation method?
Modified bitumen is best understood as:

answers.t: Asphalt-based multi-ply roofing system with polymer modifiers

questions.text: Self-adhered modified bitumen seams typically appear:

answers.t: Cleaner than torch-down or hot-mopped — no visible asphalt squeeze-out

questions.text: The primary practical advantage of self-adhered over torch-down is:

answers.t: No open flame during installation, eliminating fire risk

questions.text: When customer asks USA Flat Roof to torch-down a replacement, the rep should:

answers.t: Explain USA Flat Roof installs only self-adhered, the reasoning, and offer to quote self-adhered as alternative

questions.text: Modified bitumen blistering most commonly indicates:

answers.t: Trapped moisture or air, often from substrate condition

questions.text: Existing torch-down or hot-mopped roofs encountered on inspection:

answers.t: May be inspected and repaired with self-adhered patches; like-for-like replacement requires escalation
Should be quoted as torch-down replacement

questions.text: Modified bitumen field lifespan in NorCal is approximately:
Mid-life modified bitumen with sound substrate is best addressed with:

answers.t: Silicone restoration if it qualifies, with longer-term replacement planning

questions.text: When customer wants to compare self-adhered and torch-down replacement quote, the rep should:

answers.t: Explain the methods are different products with different fire risk profiles, costs, and considerations — not equivalent

questions.text: Acrylic coatings are positioned by USA Flat Roof as:

answers.t: Reference only — not typically proposed; USA Flat Roof leads with silicone

questions.text: Compared to silicone, acrylic coatings typically:

answers.t: Tolerate ponding water less well; tend to be lower cost; recoat more flexibly with other chemistries

questions.text: When a rep encounters existing acrylic-coated roof and customer wants silicone restoration, the rep should:

answers.t: Verify compatibility per manufacturer specifications and escalate any uncertainty

questions.text: Visual distinction between silicone and acrylic coatings is:

answers.t: Generally unreliable; documentation is the most accurate source

questions.text: Acrylic coating service life is approximately:
USA Flat Roof's primary recommendation for new restoration coating is:

answers.e: USA Flat Roof leads with silicone.

questions.text: An acrylic coating on a roof with chronic ponding is likely to:

answers.t: Wear faster in ponded areas than the rest of the roof

questions.text: Recoat compatibility from acrylic to silicone is:

answers.t: Possible but requires verification per manufacturer specifications

questions.text: USA Flat Roof's response to a customer who specifically requests acrylic restoration is:

answers.t: Explain that USA Flat Roof leads with silicone for this application; if customer specifically wants acrylic, escalate to senior estimator

questions.text: Aluminum-pigmented asphalt coating on aged BUR or modified bitumen is best understood as:

answers.t: A 2–5 year life-extension product, not a true restoration

questions.text: On a concrete tile roof, the actual waterproofing layer is:
Concrete tiles typically last:
Underlayment beneath concrete tiles typically lasts:
USA Flat Roof's scope on concrete tile work is:

answers.t: Full installation including new tile installs
Repair and maintenance only; full re-felt referred out

questions.text: Concrete vs clay tile is most reliably distinguished by:

answers.t: Tap sound — concrete dull thud, clay higher-pitched ring

questions.text: Walking on tile roofs requires:

answers.t: Walking only on the lower third of each tile, on supported areas

questions.text: Active interior leaks under a 25-year-old concrete tile roof most commonly indicate:
Mortar deterioration at the ridge is best addressed with:

answers.t: Mortar repair within USA Flat Roof's tile scope

questions.text: A customer with a 28-year-old tile roof has multiple recent leaks. The most likely diagnosis is:

answers.t: Underlayment is at end of life and full re-felt is required (referred out)

questions.text: Broken or cracked individual tiles encountered during inspection are typically addressed by:

answers.t: Replacing the broken tiles, matching profile and color where possible

questions.text: USA Flat Roof's position on installing new BUR is:

answers.t: Does not install new BUR; restores, recovers, or replaces existing BUR

questions.text: BUR is best identified by:

answers.t: Gravel surface or smooth asphalt cap, with multi-ply construction at edges

questions.text: BUR field lifespan is typically:
USA Flat Roof's recommendation framework on a single-layer BUR is:

answers.t: Silicone restoration / TPO overlay / full replacement, with SPF as fourth option

questions.text: Layer count on BUR substrates is critical because:

answers.t: Multi-layer systems often disqualify recover and require tear-off

questions.text: Pre-1980s BUR buildings require what additional inspection priority?

answers.t: Asbestos potential, layer count assessment, and substrate identification

questions.text: BUR pitch pans are most commonly addressed because:

answers.t: Sealant degrades over time and is a high-failure detail
answers.e: Pitch pan sealant is recurring maintenance.

questions.text: An aged BUR with light surface wear, intact gravel coverage, sound substrate, no asbestos suspected is best addressed with:

answers.t: Restoration coating (silicone) if it qualifies, or maintenance program

questions.text: SPF as an option on BUR substrates is appropriate when:

answers.t: When ponding correction or insulation upgrade is the goal, after gravel removal

questions.text: When a property owner asks USA Flat Roof to install new BUR, the appropriate response is:

answers.t: Explain USA Flat Roof doesn't install new BUR, present alternatives, and offer to refer to a BUR specialty contractor if they prefer like-for-like

questions.text: The most reliable visual identifier of SPF is:
The protective layer applied over cured SPF foam is called:
USA Flat Roof installs SPF for which three primary use cases?

answers.t: Ponding correction, BUR recover after gravel removal, insulation upgrade
Steep-slope shingle, parapet flashing, chimney work
Emergency leak repair, attic ventilation, skylight installation

questions.text: SPF foam itself can last:

answers.e: Foam life depends on topcoat maintenance.

questions.text: The topcoat recoat schedule on SPF is typically:

answers.t: Optional, every 30 years if at all
Mandatory every 10–15 years to maintain warranty and UV protection

questions.text: SPF can correct ponding because:

answers.t: It can be sprayed thicker in low spots and thinner near drains, creating slope during application

questions.text: Exposed foam (without topcoat) on an SPF roof typically indicates:

answers.t: Topcoat thinning or wear; UV degradation of foam if not addressed

questions.text: Walk pads on SPF roofs are installed to:

answers.t: Protect the SPF surface in high-traffic areas around equipment

questions.text: An SPF customer who skips the topcoat recoat schedule:

answers.t: Risks warranty void and UV degradation of foam, potentially shortening system life significantly
answers.e: Recoat is part of warranty maintenance.

questions.text: When SPF is recommended specifically because of ponding, the rep should also discuss:

answers.t: The alternative of replacement with tapered insulation, so customer has full picture
answers.e: Both real options for ponding correction.

questions.text: USA Flat Roof's maintenance program is positioned as:

answers.t: The structural mechanism for long-term customer relationships

questions.text: When a customer reports a leak, the inspection should:

answers.t: Focus only above the visible damage
Include the full roof, not just the leak area
Wait for customer to identify source

questions.text: The leak entry point on a roof is typically:

answers.t: Rarely directly above the interior damage; water travels along structure first

questions.text: A 24-year-old roof with three leak repairs in 18 months suggests:

answers.t: A pattern of system aging; repair-plus-plan or replacement conversation appropriate
answers.e: Pattern of failure on aged roof.

questions.text: The 'repair-plus-plan' conversation is appropriate when:

answers.t: Roof has localized failure but is at the older end of service or showing broader aging signs

questions.text: A maintenance call reveals an Urgent severity condition the rep is not authorized to address. The response is:

answers.t: Document, escalate, and communicate the finding to the customer
Quote a price on the spot

questions.text: A long-term maintenance customer's roof has reached end of life. The honest response is:

answers.t: Continue maintenance to preserve recurring revenue
Have the honest conversation: maintenance is no longer appropriate; recommend replacement options

questions.text: Service call minimums and emergency premiums:

answers.t: Should be communicated transparently in advance
Don't apply at USA Flat Roof

questions.text: When a customer says 'just patch it where it's leaking' on a 24-year-old roof, the rep should:

answers.t: Explain repeat repairs on aged roof are short-term spending; recommend inspection and present options

questions.text: Rep's response to a customer who pushes for repair-only on a clearly end-of-life roof is:

answers.t: Explain repair is short-term spending; offer phased work or honest decline if budget doesn't support replacement

questions.text: The four-point Inspection Severity Scale is:

answers.t: Monitor / Maintenance Needed / Repair Recommended / Urgent or Escalate

questions.text: The three audiences for inspection documentation are:
The four pillars of inspection are:

answers.t: Drone, photos, customer documentation, warranty documentation

questions.text: Drone inspection is most valuable for:

answers.t: Multi-story buildings, tile roofs, complex geometries where walking is risky

questions.text: Documenting ponding evidence on a dry-day inspection requires:

answers.t: Using chalk to outline extent marked by dirt rings, then photographing

questions.text: A reference scale in damage photos is:

answers.t: An object included to indicate size — business card, tape measure, chalk mark

questions.text: When a customer asks for verbal pricing during inspection, the appropriate response is:

answers.t: Defer to written proposal so scope and price match

questions.text: Reps may NOT certify which of the following?

answers.t: Structural failure, hail damage for insurance, or mold identification

questions.text: When a leak source cannot be identified after full inspection, the next step is:

answers.t: Escalate to senior estimator or qualified inspector; communicate honestly with customer

questions.text: When customer says 'documentation is too thorough — just give me a number', rep should:

answers.t: Explain documentation protects them for warranty, insurance, resale; pricing will be accurate

quizzes.chapter_title: Repair vs Restoration vs Replacement Decision Framework

questions.text: 'Repair' in the four-scope framework is best described as:

answers.t: Removing the existing roof and starting over
Addressing a specific failure on a roof with remaining service life

questions.text: 'Restoration' is best described as:

answers.t: Adding waterproofing or service life to an aged but sound substrate

questions.text: 'Recover (overlay)' is best described as:

answers.t: Installing a new system over the existing one, where code permits and substrate qualifies

questions.text: The four decision lenses for recommendations, in order, are:
Disqualifying conditions for restoration across most systems include:

answers.t: Wet insulation, severe ponding, widespread substrate failure

questions.text: A customer with a 6-year-old roof in good condition asks for replacement. The honest response is:

answers.t: Explain remaining life; recommend repair or maintenance instead
answers.e: Honest answer when customer wants more than roof needs.

questions.text: A 12-year-old TPO with single punctured area, otherwise excellent condition, is best addressed with:

answers.t: Targeted repair of puncture, with annual maintenance recommended

questions.text: Phased work is appropriate when:

answers.t: Customer's budget genuinely doesn't support full scope but the roof needs work
answers.e: Honest alternative when budget is constraint.

questions.text: A repeat customer asks for restoration on a roof the rep believes does not qualify. The response is:

answers.t: Provide because they're a repeat customer
Decline to propose restoration, explain why, recommend honest alternative

questions.text: When budget genuinely doesn't fit what the roof needs, the three honest paths are:

answers.t: Phased work, honest decline, or referral
Cut scope, cut scope, cut scope

questions.text: Balanced attic ventilation requires:

answers.t: Appropriate proportions of intake and exhaust, allowing air movement through the full attic

questions.text: Inadequate ventilation accelerates shingle aging primarily by:

answers.t: Trapping heat that ages shingles from below

questions.text: A 'short-circuit' pattern in attic ventilation occurs when:

answers.t: Two exhaust types pull from each other instead of pulling air through the full attic

questions.text: Ridge vents and gable vents on the same roof typically:

answers.t: Create a short-circuit pattern, reducing effective ventilation

questions.text: Most shingle manufacturer warranties:

answers.t: Require adequate attic ventilation; ventilation-driven aging is a common warranty denial

questions.text: Vapor and condensation issues in attics are:

answers.t: Building science issues that may exceed rep authorization and require qualified assessment

questions.text: Persistent ceiling stain inside a building with no visible roof failure suggests:

answers.t: Possible vapor, condensation, or assembly moisture issue requiring qualified assessment

questions.text: Attic frost on the deck during cold conditions indicates:

answers.t: Inadequate ventilation allowing moisture to condense

questions.text: When a homeowner asks the rep to skip ventilation upgrade on re-roof to save cost, the response is:

answers.t: Explain ventilation drives roof life and warranty coverage; document if customer still declines

questions.text: Flat roof condensation issues are typically driven by:

answers.t: Vapor moving from interior into the assembly, not roof leaks; requires assembly-level diagnosis

questions.text: Most roof leaks happen at:
Pipe boots most commonly fail because:

answers.t: UV degradation cracking the rubber, particularly at the top where the boot wraps the pipe

questions.text: A 'kick-out' flashing is:

answers.t: A small piece at the bottom of step flashing that diverts water away from the wall and into the gutter

questions.text: Step flashing is found at:

answers.t: The sidewalls of sloped roofs, with each piece overlapping the one below

questions.text: Counter-flashing is installed:

answers.t: Over a base flashing (such as step flashing) to direct water away and protect the joint

questions.text: Pitch pans require regular attention because:

answers.t: Their sealant degrades over time, making them a high-failure detail

questions.text: Drip edge installed behind the gutter (instead of correctly positioned) creates:

answers.t: Water that runs down the fascia behind the gutter, leading to fascia rot and roof edge issues

questions.text: Skylight perimeter flashings most commonly leak because of:

answers.t: Aging perimeter seal, frame seal, or improper installation

questions.text: A customer dismisses detail-focused inspection as 'looking for problems'. The honest response is:

answers.t: Explain that details are where roofs typically leak; offer the customer the choice with caveats
Tell them they need no work

questions.text: Standard flashing scope on re-roof projects typically includes:

answers.t: Replacement of all flashings, with reuse only where conditions support it

questions.text: The industry standard threshold for acceptable ponding is:

answers.t: Up to 24 hours after rain stops
Up to 48 hours after rain stops

questions.text: Which is NOT a flat-roof drainage approach?
An overflow drain is:

answers.t: A secondary drainage point at higher elevation, intended to handle water if primary clogs

questions.text: A 'ponding ring' is:

answers.t: Dirt accumulation pattern marking where water has been sitting

questions.text: A silicone restoration coating applied to a chronically ponded roof will:

answers.t: Fix the ponding by adding slope
Not fix the ponding — coatings don't add slope; coating will likely fail over the ponded area
Last longer than on dry areas

questions.text: Which roof system can correct ponding through tapered application during installation?
When a property manager reports water 'always sits in that one spot', the appropriate first response is:

answers.t: Tell them flat roofs always pond
Inspect, document ponding evidence, verify drainage and overflow

questions.text: Most likely cause of ponding on a 30-year-old roof that originally drained well:

answers.t: Building settlement, deck deflection, or insulation compression over time

questions.text: A customer wants the cheapest option to address chronic ponding. The honest options are:

answers.t: SPF or replacement with tapered insulation; both address slope

questions.text: A customer dismisses ponding because 'another contractor said it's fine'. The response is:

answers.t: Tell customer the other contractor was wrong
Document what you observed, explain the 48-hour standard, let customer compare

questions.text: The first question on every commercial inspection is:

answers.t: What roof system is on the building?

questions.text: A white commercial roof with welded seams is most likely:
A black single-ply with glued or taped seams is most likely:
A flat roof with characteristic orange-peel surface texture is:
When a rep cannot certify TPO vs PVC, the appropriate response is:

answers.t: Confirm PVC based on slick feel
Document visual indicators; note product type cannot be certified without documentation

questions.text: The most reliable way to identify SPF vs silicone-coated single-ply is:
A property manager says 'we have TPO'. Rep observes characteristics suggesting PVC. Response:

answers.t: Document, ask for documentation, escalate if scope depends on certainty

questions.text: On a heavily coated commercial roof, the most important identification step is:
A 1968 commercial building with white-coated flat roof creates which inspection priorities?

answers.t: Asbestos potential, layer count assessment, substrate identification

questions.text: A restaurant with significant kitchen exhaust shows white welded-seam membrane that has held up well around grease vents:

answers.e: Restaurant + grease + held up well = PVC.

questions.text: Most reliable indicator that a residential roof is composition shingle is:

answers.e: None of the look-alikes have granular surface.

questions.text: Concrete vs clay tile most reliably differs in:

answers.t: Tap sound — concrete dull thud, clay higher-pitched ring

questions.text: Architectural shingle is best described as:

answers.t: Two layers bonded together creating shadowed, textured appearance

questions.text: USA Flat Roof's residential scope includes:

answers.t: All residential systems including slate and wood shake
Composition shingle (full installation, repair, replacement) and concrete tile (repair and maintenance)

questions.text: A 1995 NorCal tract home with red barrel-profile roof is most likely:

answers.t: Concrete tile (pigmented or coated to mimic clay)
answers.e: Era and tract pattern in NorCal.

questions.text: Pieces that look like wood but tap with plastic pop are:
A 1925 Berkeley craftsman with thin gray tiles, natural stone variation, ringing tap is:

answers.e: Era, location, ring, natural variation match.

questions.text: Customer reports their roof as 'tile' without specifying. The most appropriate next step is:

answers.t: Inspect to identify whether concrete or clay (and profile), since scope depends on it
Refer to tile contractor without inspection

questions.text: Home with pitched composition shingle main roof and flat-roof addition. Flat section needs:

answers.t: No inspection — flat sections don't matter

questions.text: Customer says 'real slate'. Inspection shows uniform manufactured color, light weight, plastic-pop tap. Response:

answers.t: Document observed (synthetic appearance), explain indicators, ask for documentation
Quote as real slate based on report
